message:
feat(robot): register is-working capability, extract help sections into testable data, skip slow tests in -short mode

Add the "is-working" command to buildCommandRegistry() so it appears
in --robot-capabilities output with its session/panes/verbose params
and three usage examples.

Refactor PrintHelp() to pull inline command-section definitions into
a package-level robotHelpSections slice of robotHelpSection structs
(Title + SurfaceNames). Change renderHelpCommandSection() to accept
the struct. Five sections defined: Core Commands, Session Operations,
Work Distribution, Analysis & Monitoring, and Tool Bridges.

Add TestPrintHelp_CommandSectionsResolveAgainstRegistry to verify
every surface name in every help section resolves against the live
RobotRegistry, catching help-vs-registry drift at test time.

Introduce skipSlowRobotShortIntegrationTest() helper and wire it
into 14 slow integration tests across robot_test.go, robot_extra_test.go,
service_layer_test.go, and tui_parity_test.go so "go test -short"
stays fast while the full suite still runs in CI.

Update GetRobotRegistry() doc comment to reflect detached-snapshot
semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+type robotHelpSection struct {

+	SurfaceNames []string
+	Postlude     string
+}

+var robotHelpSections = []robotHelpSection{

+                        Note: bead_orphaned is deliberately unsupported — see --robot-capabilities

+Note: Pane-targeting commands exclude the user pane by default.
+Use --all to include the user pane (index depends on tmux pane-base-index).

+}
